top_stories.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
top_stories.summary: A novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing is being actively exploited by at least two threat actors to validate stolen credentials in Microsoft Entra ID environments. The attack allows adversaries to enumerate user accounts and verify stolen credentials without generating successful sign-in events, effectively bypassing standard telemetry and defender alerts.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID gets passkeys default authentication starting September
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for Entra ID starting September 2026. This represents a major shift in enterprise authentication policy for one of the most widely deployed identity platforms, moving away from traditional password-based methods by default.

top_stories.title: Millions of Microsoft Entra Accounts Targeted in OAuth Client ID Spoofing Campaigns
top_stories.summary: Proofpoint has detailed an attack campaign targeting millions of Microsoft Entra accounts through OAuth client ID spoofing techniques. The attackers use spoofed OAuth client IDs to probe accounts, test credentials, and evade standard sign-in detection mechanisms at cloud scale.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID authentication overhaul to start in September 2026
top_stories.summary: Microsoft will roll out passkeys as the default authentication experience for Microsoft Entra ID in the public cloud beginning September 1, 2026. Organizations with SMS or voice authentication enabled will be automatically enabled for passkeys, marking a significant shift in default authentication behavior across Entra ID.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID to enforce passkeys and retire legacy MFA by 2027
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra ID will make passkeys the default authentication method for all public cloud tenants starting September 1, 2026, with full retirement of legacy SMS and voice-based MFA by 2027. Users currently relying on those legacy MFA methods will be automatically transitioned as part of this enforced platform-wide authentication policy change.

news.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
news.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in authentication security.

news.title: Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
news.summary: Zoom disclosed a critical vulnerability in its Windows desktop client and SDK that allows unauthenticated attackers to take over user accounts. The flaw represents an authentication bypass risk affecting a widely deployed collaboration platform.

news.title: Socure rolls out Remote Verifier for higher-risk identity checks
news.summary: Socure has launched Remote Verifier within its RiskOS platform, an AI-powered identity verification tool targeting individuals who fail initial document verification checks. The solution claims to verify over 99% of identities on the first attempt, compared to an industry average of 64%, reducing manual review requirements for higher-risk identity checks.

news.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
news.summary: SonicWall has disclosed two actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in its Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one rated CVSS 10.0. The critical flaw (CVE-2026-15409) is a server-side request forgery vulnerability exploitable by remote unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary commands.

news.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
news.summary: Microsoft released its largest-ever Patch Tuesday update, covering 622 CVEs including two zero-days already under active exploitation. The release is more than triple the previous record of approximately 200 CVEs from a single month.

news.title: Microsoft is forcing an enterprise transition to passkeys
news.summary: Microsoft Entra ID will replace SMS and voice authentication with passkeys beginning in September. The change affects enterprise authentication flows and reflects a policy shift away from traditional credential methods in response to AI-powered attack threats.

news.title: SailPoint、パートナーのイノベーションを促進する「ユニファイド プラットフォーム アクセス」プログラムを発表
news.summary: SailPoint announced a new program called "Unified Platform Access" aimed at accelerating partner innovation. The program was originally announced in the United States on June 16, 2026, with this being a Japanese-language summary translation of that release.

news.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
news.summary: Two access control vulnerabilities in RabbitMQ have been disclosed, allowing attackers to leak OAuth client secrets and bypass cross-tenant queue boundaries. The flaws were discovered by Miggo's security team and affect RabbitMQ's OAuth integration and enterprise messaging infrastructure access controls.

news.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
news.summary: Microsoft has identified three attack paths used by threat actors aligned with ShinyHunters to compromise Salesforce environments over the past year. The attacks exploit OAuth connections and trusted third-party integrations rather than platform vulnerabilities, highlighting risks in federated identity and authorization chains.

news.title: Configure Microsoft Entra Backup and Recovery
news.summary: Microsoft Entra Backup and Recovery reached general availability on June 30, 2026, as a built-in solution for Microsoft Entra ID. The feature automatically backs up critical directory objects daily, enabling restoration to a previous state after accidental changes or deletions.

news.title: New phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts, evade MFA
news.summary: Two new phishing kits, Jalisco and OmegaLord, have been discovered targeting Microsoft 365 accounts using techniques that bypass multi-factor authentication. The kits employ adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) or similar MFA-defeating methods to compromise accounts despite MFA protections being in place.

news.title: Zero trust workload identity manager version 1.1 generally available on Red Hat OpenShift
news.summary: A zero trust workload identity manager has reached version 1.1 general availability on Red Hat OpenShift, addressing identity and access management challenges in multi-cluster, multi-cloud, and hybrid environments. The solution targets limitations of traditional security mechanisms such as long-lived secrets, static certificates, and cloud provider-specific IAM systems.

news.title: Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called Forg365 targets Microsoft 365 accounts using device code phishing and adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) session theft techniques. The service, distributed via Telegram for $400/month, combines antibot evasion, AI-assisted lure creation, and post-compromise mailbox operations to bypass authentication controls.

news.title: Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
news.summary: An unknown threat actor used a suspected AI-generated PowerShell script to enumerate Active Directory environments, mapping users, computers, and domains before exporting data into a report file. The attack highlights emerging use of AI-assisted tooling for AD reconnaissance, targeting a core identity infrastructure component.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA published a postmortem after a contractor exposed dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management failures and gaps in secret scanning and response processes relevant to IAM and secrets management practices.

news.title: AI agents are becoming the enterprise's most privileged users. And most organisations don't know it yet
news.summary: AI agents are increasingly operating with highly privileged access within enterprise environments, often without adequate identity governance controls in place. Most organizations have not yet recognized or addressed the identity and access management risks posed by autonomous AI agents acting as privileged users.

news.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of managing AI agents as a distinct identity type within IAM frameworks. It argues that traditional identity constructs like service accounts and API tokens are insufficient for governing AI agent identities, requiring new approaches to authentication and authorization.

news.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
news.summary: A cryptomining incident demonstrated how AI gateways can be exploited to gain access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and IAM data. The attack vector highlights emerging security risks where AI gateway compromises can expose identity and access management systems and credentials.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London, on the first day of what was scheduled to be a six-week trial. Scattered Spider is a cybercrime group known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and gain unauthorized access to corporate systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, briefly compromising high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication and trigger password resets were shared on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.
